full_text:
A severe heatwave continues to scorch Maharashtra’s Vidarbha region, with temperatures crossing 45 degrees Celsius in several districts.
A severe heatwave continues to grip Maharashtra’s Vidarbha region, with temperatures soaring above 45 degrees Celsius in several districts. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for Akola, Amravati, and Wardha, warning that temperatures could touch 47 degrees Celsius in the coming days.
According to weather officials in Nagpur, no major change in weather conditions is expected over the next five to seven days, keeping the region under intense heat stress.
Residents in Nagpur are taking precautions to cope with the extreme weather by staying hydrated, covering their heads, and avoiding outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours. Many commuters and field workers are relying on buttermilk, lemon water, fruits, and energy drinks to prevent dehydration.
To provide some relief to commuters, Nagpur authorities have installed green shade nets at key traffic intersections, helping shield people from the scorching sun while waiting at traffic signals.

title: Mumbai weather today: Sticky pre-monsoon heat continues as IMD issues heatwave, rain alerts in Maharashtra - News9live
full_text:
Mumbai weather today: Sticky pre-monsoon heat continues as IMD issues heatwave, rain alerts in Maharashtra
Mumbai's sticky pre-monsoon weather continues as IMD predicts cloudy skies, high humidity, and light showers for today. Meanwhile, severe heatwave red alerts lock down Vidarbha districts, including Akola and Amravati.
New Delhi: The weather in the Mumbai remains sticky, uncomfortable pre-monsoon heat with no immediate respite in sight. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), moisture in the air is making conditions uncomfortable, especially in coastal parts of Maharashtra.
IMD has warned citizens that heavy coastal humidity continues to make ambient conditions feel significantly hotter than actual thermometer readings. The officials said the southwest monsoon is progressing normally but it is expected to reach Vidarbha only during the second week of June So till then, hot weather conditions are likely to continue in the region.
On Wednesday, the financial capital recorded partly cloudy skies throughout the day. The maximum temperature was around 33 degrees Celsius, while the minimum settled at 27 degrees Celsius.
Mumbai weather forecast today
According to the Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC), Mumbai and surrounding areas are likely to witness partly cloudy skies again on Thursday. The maximum temperature may rise to around 35 degrees Celsius.
Humidity levels are expected to remain high, making the weather feel warmer than the actual temperature. Some areas may receive light rain or brief showers, but no major drop in temperature is expected for now.
Maharashtra weather update
The weather in Maharashtra has been changing continuously for the past few days, with some areas experiencing extreme heat while others are having trouble with heavy rains. The IMD has issued a yellow rain alert for Sindhudurg, Kolhapur, and nearby ghat regions.
A heatwave red alert has been issued for Akola, Amravati, and Wardha, while an orange alert is in place for Nagpur, Chandrapur, and Yavatmal.
Over the next few days, light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely to occur at isolated places in the districts of South Konkan-Goa and South Madhya Maharashtra.
When will Mumbai get relief from the heat?
The residents eagerly waiting for respite from the sticky conditions will not have to wait much longer. According to the IMD, the southwest monsoon is likely to reach Mumbai around June 5 or 6, provided weather conditions remain favourable.
While pre-monsoon showers are expected to bring temporary relief across parts of Maharashtra later this week, the financial capital will officially welcome the monsoon during the first week of June if the current forecast holds steady.

title: Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: Humid Heat Persists as IMD Issues Rain and Heatwave Alerts Across Maharashtra - Asianet Newsable
full_text:
Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: Humid Heat Persists as IMD Issues Rain and Heatwave Alerts Across Maharashtra
Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: Mumbai continues to battle sticky pre-monsoon heat as humidity levels soar across the city. While light showers may offer brief relief, several Maharashtra districts remain under heatwave and rain alerts issued by IMD
Mumbai Continues to Sweat Under Humid Pre-Monsoon Conditions
Mumbai residents are dealing with another day of uncomfortable weather as high humidity levels continue to make temperatures feel harsher than they actually are. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the city is likely to witness partly cloudy skies on Thursday, with temperatures expected to touch around 35 degrees Celsius.
Even though the recorded temperature may not appear extreme, the moisture in the air is increasing discomfort, especially in coastal areas. On Wednesday, Mumbai experienced partly cloudy weather with temperatures ranging between 27 and 33 degrees Celsius.
The Regional Meteorological Centre has also hinted at the possibility of light rain or short showers in isolated pockets. However, these brief spells are unlikely to bring any major cooling effect for now.
IMD Issues Heatwave and Rain Alerts Across Maharashtra
Weather conditions across Maharashtra remain sharply divided. While coastal and ghat regions may receive rainfall, parts of Vidarbha are facing intense heatwave conditions.
The IMD has issued a red alert for Akola, Amravati, and Wardha due to severe heatwave conditions. At the same time, orange alerts remain in place for Nagpur, Chandrapur, and Yavatmal, where temperatures continue to remain dangerously high.
Meanwhile, districts such as Sindhudurg, Kolhapur, and nearby ghat regions are under a yellow alert for rainfall. Light to moderate showers and isolated thunderstorms are also expected in parts of South Konkan-Goa and South Madhya Maharashtra over the coming days.
When Will Mumbai Finally Get Relief?
There is some good news for residents waiting for cooler weather. The southwest monsoon is progressing steadily and is expected to arrive in Mumbai around June 5 or 6, if current weather patterns remain favourable.
Before the official monsoon onset, some pre-monsoon showers are likely to occur later this week and may provide temporary relief from the heat and humidity. However, until the monsoon fully advances into the region, Mumbai is expected to continue experiencing warm and sticky conditions.
